review_text_raw: I initially contacted Morgan & Morgan about a month ago regarding an injury I sustained after being burned at Cuts Steakhouse in Suwanee. I provided all of my information and was told they would start my case. After waiting a full month without any communication or updates, I reached out to ask for a status update. Today, I received a call from one of their attorneys, Alex Griffin. He stated that because the case would likely only be worth around $3,000?$5,000, they may not be able to take it due to the recovery of their fees. I explained that I had first tried to resolve my medical bills on my own directly with Cuts Steakhouse, but received no response, which is why I sought legal help. His response was essentially, "You were injured, but it?s not enough for us to take on." He showed no empathy, became irritated when I asked questions, and was rude and disrespectful. He eventually said the call was "going nowhere," told me he would reach back out in February, and then hung up on me. I called Morgan & Morgan back to report the interaction and requested another attorney. I made a formal report, and less than an hour later, Alex Griffin left me a voicemail stating that he was dropping my case. At no point was I rude or raised my voice?he was clearly irritated simply because I was asking questions. Morgan & Morgan advertises that they are "for the people," but my experience was extremely uncomfortable and disappointing. I will never use them again, and based on how I was treated, I would not recommend them to anyone else.

review_text_raw: Mr. Garcia did a great job defending me in court and assisting me in receiving my restraining order. The only issue with his services was the time he took to file my court documents. Our last court date was in early December, when I was granted my restraining order, and he did not file it with the court until the first week of February. I know holidays made things delayed, but he could've filed the documents the same day of our last hearing. His justification was that the court gave him issues filing, which I honestly don't believe. review_text_raw:
Disappointing Experience - Beware of Misleading Positive Reviews

Despite the many glowing reviews, my experience with New Frontier was extremely disappointing. We hired them for a straightforward Permanent Resident card renewal, but the process was mishandled at every stage:

Delayed submission: Our application was submitted over 60 days late, with no valid explanation despite all fees being paid and documents provided upfront.
Incorrect forms: They submitted the wrong application, resulting in a denial. Initially, they tried to charge us a resubmission fee for their own error, only reversing this after I insisted.
Poor communication: Scheduled meetings were missed at least five times, and I only received responses after repeatedly following up.
Lack of transparency: Important correspondence was never shared proactively. Critical documents were withheld for over 60 days, impacting our ability to renew license.
Neglect of details: The online access code was never sent, and by the time I asked, it had expired-creating further unnecessary work.

Given these repeated issues, I am genuinely skeptical of the overwhelmingly positive reviews. Our simple case became a source of frustration and wasted time. I strongly advise others to look elsewhere for reliable immigration services.

I am taking a huge risk here as my Moms case is still open with them but I simply cannot in good faith hold off until the process is complete. Stay away... And if someone from this office comments and tells me to call them.. I have very frequently and nothing changed. Spoke to the actual Attorney handling the case and nothing changed. Disgusted to say the least. If this is how they treat their customers, many who may not question them and think this is normal they should not be serving our community.
review_text_normalized: disappointing experience - beware of misleading positive reviews despite the many glowing reviews, my experience with new frontier was extremely disappointing. we hired them for a straightforward permanent resident card renewal, but the process was mishandled at every stage: delayed submission: our application was submitted over 60 days late, with no valid explanation despite all fees being paid and documents provided upfront. incorrect forms: they submitted the wrong application, resulting in a denial. initially, they tried to charge us a resubmission fee for their own error, only reversing this after i insisted. poor communication: scheduled meetings were missed at least five times, and i only received responses after repeatedly following up. lack of transparency: important correspondence was never shared proactively. critical documents were withheld for over 60 days, impacting our ability to renew license. neglect of details: the online access code was never sent, and by the time i asked, it had expired-creating further unnecessary work. given these repeated issues, i am genuinely skeptical of the overwhelmingly positive reviews. our simple case became a source of frustration and wasted time. i strongly advise others to look elsewhere for reliable immigration services. i am taking a huge risk here as my moms case is still open with them but i simply cannot in good faith hold off until the process is complete. stay away... and if someone from this office comments and tells me to call them.. i have very frequently and nothing changed. spoke to the actual attorney handling the case and nothing changed. disgusted to say the least. if this is how they treat their customers, many who may not question them and think this is normal they should not be serving our community.

review_text_raw: Hello, good afternoon. Today I was in his office at 10:30 am, I wanted to speak with my Lawyer Alexander and they denied me to speak with him. He was assisted by his personal secretary and made me go to a conference office and he told me that Alexander was no longer my Lawyer that he canceled the contract and did not want to represent me anymore. I told her that I wanted to talk to him, Attorney Alexander, and she told me that he did not want to talk to me and wanted to give me the contract cancellation papers, but I did not accept them and she told me that she did not care because she already sent him mail. I told him that he called me on Friday afternoon but I was finished at the hospital Mountain View and I could not answer when he called because the doctors were checking me out. They also left me hospitalized and the next day they did an Emergency MRI at 8am due to the condition of my condition due to the very high Pressure. Nausea, a lot of headache, blurred vision, dizziness, etc. I called after they did the MRI at around 9am. I talked to the operator because she told me that the offices were locked. I told her that I wanted to speak with my Lawyer Alexander and she took all my information and told me that she would send him a message immediately so that Lawyer Alexander could contact me. I told her that I was admitted to the Mountain View Hospital and she also told her I passed Room 504 where I was. Because I wanted to explain everything the doctors told me about my critical situation and for that reason I was hospitalized. But Lawyer Alexander never called me or even sent me messages. Today I went to his office, Erick Palacios, to speak with the lawyer and they denied me. Until today, Tuesday July 30, it is 12:40 pm. I never heard back from Attorney Alexander either by phone or by message. I signed a contract with the Lawyer for him to be my representative and he told me that I had peace of mind and was in very good hands. In addition, they charge me 33 percent of the case when they win plus administrative expenses, etc. You, Mr. Erick Palacios, brought me a case in December 2019 and the marriage was completed and closed in December 2021 and you paid me compensation for my injury and you collected all the expenses and made money with my Trajo accident More than 25,000 Dollars you earned with me. Now I want Mr. Eric Palacios because they are not treating me with the education and seriousness that I deserve as a loyal client of his Law Firm. I returned for the second time to have you represent me, but the Legal Assistant Magaly treated me very badly, disrespecting me for a telephone conversation we had last week when I asked her to please send me all the medical papers that you made copies and some photos of my accident that I texted to his office. I explained to her that she needed them to send it by email or fax to the phone number that I sent her by message and the photo of the card from the company where I work. She refused to send those photos and told me that I had to go to an office and send it by email. Because this was not her job. So I want you, Eric Palacios, to explain to me why you are charging me 33 percent plus administrative expenses, when your legal assistants are not doing their job. I need an appointment with you to discuss this issue personally. I thank you in advance for your attention. Sincerely Gabriel Horacio Rodriguez....July 30 at 12:58 pm I am sending this message by text to the number that Lawyer Alexander gave me, thank you very much and good afternoon...

review_text_raw:
I could not be happier with the services I received from the Fulton Law Office. I initially used their services for a workman's compensation claim. Once Mr. Fulton took over, I breathed a sigh of relief. He answered all my questions, explained everything so I could understand and kept in communication with me. My case was approved! Everyone I encountered or spoke to there was pleasant and professional.

When I needed their services again for a Social Security Disability claim, Chelsea Rubin and Kay Sanchez were amazing. They handled obtaining all my medical records and submitting them to Social Security. If I had a concern or question, even over a weekend, Kay would respond to me quickly! They were thorough and efficient! I could not have handled this without them. Because of their hard work, my Disability claim was approved within 5 month, which is very quick!

I would highly recommend them for any workman's compensation or Social Security Disability claims! At a time when I was trying to handle some very stressful issues, they took the stress away. Can't thank them all enough!

review_text_raw:
I am deeply disappointed with my recent experience with this law firm. Somewhere around Five years ago, I had a positive experience with a founding attorney at this firm, receiving valuable advice. And felt like the attorney had a great moral compass.
When I encountered another legal matter, I returned to the firm, which had relocated to a new building.

I was referred to a different attorney in the practice, and paid a $250 consultation fee and met with the attorneys to discuss potential options. I was subsequently provided with a pay link for the retainer. I felt like it was a great consultation with the attorneys and highly considered retaining them.
However, due to the holiday season and increased workload, I had to postpone the matter.
Upon resuming the matter in the new year, I contacted the law firm to inquire about the possibility of asking a question before paying the retainer.
The secretary informed me that I would need to pay an additional $250 to ask a single question, then it will additionally be the $2500 retainer fee, equating to a total of $3000 out-of-pocket, before even utilizing the attorney's services.

I requested that the secretary, please take down my information and request, and forward it to the attorney to allow him the decision of whether or not he would be willing to answer my question.
However, I was denied this request.

The secretary insisted that I pay another $250 to do a full consult with the attorney and was unwilling to pass on my request. And was subsequently told "that's the way we do things."

This situation demonstrates a clear prioritization of financial gain over the establishment of a professional client relationship.

I had only one question to ask before paying the substantial amount for a retainer, yet I was met with a demand for yet another payment.

While I acknowledge the positive interaction I had during my initial consultation, (which is why I chose two stars instead of one) I am deeply disappointed with the way the secretary handled the situation.

I initially chose this firm because it was a Christian-owned firm that I thought placed moral values above all else. However, it is now perceived that the firm's emphasis is solely on financial gain above anything else.

It feels that the firm has deviated from its original principles. As a result of this experience, I am beyond disappointed.
